name: Traditional Mexican Molletes
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). Line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper to catch any drips.

2. Slice the Bolillo Rolls in half lengthwise. Remove some of the soft bread from the middle of each roll half to create a shallow well that can accommodate more toppings. This helps prevent the toppings from sliding off.

3. Spread the Butter evenly on each bread half, making sure to cover the entire surface. The butter will help create a golden, crispy base.

4. Spread the warmed Refried Beans generously on each buttered bread half, filling the hollowed area and spreading to the edges.

5. Shred the Cheddar Cheese and sprinkle it generously over the beans on each molletes, covering them completely.

6. Place the prepared bread halves on the baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven until the cheese is melted and bubbly, and the edges of the bread are golden and crispy, 15 to 20 minutes. The cheese should be lightly browned in spots.

7. Remove from the oven and top each molletes with Salsa before serving hot.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat oven and line baking sheet with parchment paper.

2. Slice Bolillo Rolls in half and hollow out the center to make room for toppings.

3. Spread Butter on each bread half.

4. Spread warmed Refried Beans generously on the buttered bread.

5. Top with shredded Cheddar Cheese and bake until melted and crispy.

6. Serve hot with Salsa on top.
